description:
Goal
Replace the hanging TODO Enum tier0 drive test with bounded convergence evidence based on a clean rebuild. The current fresh-build failure is still an unbounded drive: the exact lib test does not finish within 30s, and the fresh `fz2 build` path burns the compiler2 5s drive budget on the fixture with thousands of jobs still in flight.

Signal
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 30s target/debug/deps/fz-01415279b97da685 --exact compiler2::port_codegen_test::enum_tier0_fixture_exercises_basic_operations --nocapture` exits before timeout.
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 10s target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry <log> build fixtures2/00277_enum_tier0_fixture.fz -o <out>` exits before the compiler2 5s drive limit.
- Telemetry gives a compact event budget and identifies the remaining Enum operation or activation frontier instead of leaving the TODO test as an unbounded smoke run.
- The fix does not solve this by increasing timeouts, swallowing `drive()` again, or adding fixture-specific Enum branches.

Strategy
Use the fresh `fz2 build` timeout as the ground truth: `2387` jobs, `4` pending, stopped in semantic closure. Identify the frontier that keeps `SealSemanticClosure(RootId(0))` rearming new executables, then either finish the missing Enum semantic path or split a narrower blocking ticket with that exact frontier.

Outro
No unbounded TODO test, no swallowed drive hang, no fixture-wide special case, no broad top/bottom readiness sentinel, and no ticket text that mistakes this fresh-build timeout for a completed fast-fail.
title: Compiler2 Enum tier0: replace hanging TODO drive with bounded convergence evidence
